name: Jackson Bus Line
recent_news: No public news found; very small operator with ~25 employees and ~$1.1M revenue based in North Augusta SC area serving GA/SC market.
fit_notes: Micro-fleet, no web presence and minimal public footprint. Likely too small to be a meaningful Summit tuck-in unless used to backfill a route gap in the Augusta/Aiken corridor.

name: Go Riteway
recent_news: Celebrated 60 years in business (2019). Bob Zanotti named first non-family president/CEO in 2017. Owns Cardinal Buses (IN) subsidiary. Multi-state footprint across WI, MI, IN.
fit_notes: High-quality family-owned regional operator with diversified school bus + charter/coach mix across WI/MI/IN. Multi-generation Bast family ownership with installed professional CEO suggests succession-planning posture - strong potential Summit platform/tuck-in target, though likely larger than the dataset's '18 employees' estimate suggests (employee count appears materially understated).

name: Justranzit School Bus Inc
recent_news: FL corporation administratively dissolved 09/27/2024 for failure to file annual report. USDOT 2901693 registered as Non-Emergency Medical Transport (van) and school bus carrier in Fort Pierce FL.
fit_notes: Not a viable tuck-in: Florida entity administratively dissolved Sept 2024, micro-scale NEMT/van operator rather than yellow-bus contractor. Skip.

name: Pioneer Transportation Corp
recent_news: ~$9M EPA Clean School Bus Rebate award to deploy 25 new electric school buses in Bronx District 7. Named in 2024 NY AG settlement (with three other NYC contractors) over illegal idling at bus yards.
fit_notes: Sizable NYC DOE school-bus contractor (~40,000 students/day, 50+ years operating, Staten Island + Bronx yards). Independent private ownership not disclosed publicly - high-value potential NYC platform/scale tuck-in for Summit, with credible EV transition story already in motion. Pre-LOI diligence needed on owners, union exposure, and NYC contract renewal cycle. Note revenue_est=0 and 'single' size tier in source row are clearly inaccurate.

name: Lakeside Transportation
owner_or_parent: First Student (EQT Infrastructure) - via Cook-Illinois Corporation
districts_or_customers: Chicago-area school districts (Waukegan, Mundelein, Libertyville and surrounding)
recent_news: Parent Cook-Illinois Corporation (and all 16 subsidiaries including Lakeside Transportation and Kickert) sold to First Student in early 2026. Cook-Illinois operated ~2,200-2,300 buses across the Chicago metro and was the largest family-owned US school bus contractor prior to sale.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Lakeside Transportation is a Cook-Illinois subsidiary, and Cook-Illinois was acquired by First Student (EQT-owned) in Feb 2026 - now sits inside Summit's largest strategic competitor. Remove from target list.
